req.title: Hr-Admin deletes performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a performance-review through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path deletion by a valid hr-admin (EP – valid equivalence class), (2) unauthenticated access returning 401 (NEG), (3) forbidden role returning 403 (NEG/DT), (4) validation error on missing mandatory field returning 400 (BVA – empty/null boundary), and (5) state transition from 'existing review' → 'deleted review' with audit record (STC). Five tests are generated to achieve the minimum coverage mandated and to address all cited error pathways.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_performance_review_success
preconditions: A performance-review record exists with a known ID
actions: Submit the delete form with valid mandatory fields
expected: HTTP response 200 or 201 with the deleted entity id
Performance-review no longer appears in the listing
An audit record is written for the deletion event
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_deletes_performance_review_success(hr_admin_page, existing_review_id, audit_log_client):
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/performance-reviews")
    <helper_fn: wait_for_review_row>(page, existing_review_id)

    delete_btn = page.locator(f"[data-review-id='{existing_review_id}'] [data-action='delete']")
    delete_btn.click()

    <helper_fn: fill_delete_confirmation_form>(page, existing_review_id)

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/performance-reviews" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")) as resp_info:
        page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete-btn']").click()

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(existing_review_id)

    page.reload()
    expect(page.locator(f"[data-review-id='{existing_review_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_review_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_performance_review
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
A performance-review record exists with a known ID
actions: Send DELETE request to /performance-reviews/{id} without authentication headers
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_performance_review(base_url, existing_review_id, audit_log_client):
    url = f"{base_url}/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}"

    resp = requests.delete(url, headers={}, timeout=10)

    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_review_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must not be written for unauthenticated request"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_performance_review
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
A performance-review record exists with a known ID
actions: Send DELETE request to /performance-reviews/{id} with employee-role auth token
trace: R-WEB-023: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
R-WEB-023: only hr-admin actor is authorised
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_performance_review(base_url, employee_auth_headers, existing_review_id, audit_log_client):
    url = f"{base_url}/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}"

    resp = requests.delete(url, headers=employee_auth_headers, timeout=10)

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_review_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must not be written for forbidden role"

tests.name: test_delete_with_empty_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: A performance-review record exists with a known ID
actions: Open the delete form for an existing review
Clear the mandatory confirmation field (empty string – boundary of valid input)
expected: Validation error message is displayed for the empty mandatory field
trace: R-WEB-023: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-023: error pathway – 400 on validation error
BVA: empty string is the lower boundary of the mandatory text field
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_delete_with_empty_mandatory_field_returns_400(hr_admin_page, existing_review_id, audit_log_client):
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/performance-reviews")
    <helper_fn: wait_for_review_row>(page, existing_review_id)

    page.locator(f"[data-review-id='{existing_review_id}'] [data-action='delete']").click()

    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-field']").fill("")

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/performance-reviews" in r.url) as resp_info:
        page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete-btn']").click()

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status}"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='field-error']")).to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_review_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must not be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_delete_transitions_review_state_to_deleted
preconditions: A performance-review exists in state ACTIVE with a known ID
actions: Assert review is in ACTIVE state via API before deletion
Submit valid delete action through the UI
Assert review state via API after deletion
expected: Post-condition: review state is DELETED or resource returns 404
State transition ACTIVE → DELETED is recorded in audit log
trace: R-WEB-023: audit record written on success
STC: ACTIVE → DELETED state transition coverage
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_transitions_review_state_to_deleted(hr_admin_page, base_url, hr_admin_api_headers, existing_review_id, audit_log_client):
    pre = requests.get(f"{base_url}/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}", headers=hr_admin_api_headers, timeout=10)
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json().get("status") == "ACTIVE", "Pre-condition: review must be ACTIVE"

    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/performance-reviews")
    <helper_fn: wait_for_review_row>(page, existing_review_id)
    page.locator(f"[data-review-id='{existing_review_id}'] [data-action='delete']").click()
    <helper_fn: fill_delete_confirmation_form>(page, existing_review_id)
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete-btn']").click()
    <helper_fn: wait_for_success_toast>(page)

    post = requests.get(f"{base_url}/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}", headers=hr_admin_api_headers, timeout=10)
    assert post.status_code in (200, 404)
    if post.status_code == 200:
        assert post.json().get("status") == "DELETED", "Post-condition: review must be DELETED"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_review_id, action="DELETE")
    assert any(e.get("from_state") == "ACTIVE" and e.get("to_state") == "DELETED" for e in audit_entries), "State-transition audit record missing"
